symptoms.symptom_name: Acute aphonia (complete voice loss) developing over hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vocal-cord oedema impairing phonation is the defining feature of laryngitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preceding coryzal illness (rhinorrhoea, fatigue) for three days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A viral prodrome preceding laryngeal symptoms points to a viral aetiology.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: vocal cord structural lesion (nodule or polyp)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An acute infective onset argues against a chronic structural cause of hoarseness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry non-productive tickling cough with retching

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Laryngeal irritation produces a dry irritant cough; the absence of purulent sputum argues against a bacterial lower-respiratory cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild odynophagia from forceful throat clearing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild mechanical throat soreness is non-specific; severe odynophagia would instead raise epiglottitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of stridor, drooling, dysphagia for saliva and high fever (negative airway red-flag screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: These negatives are what allow epiglottitis to be excluded remotely and reassurance to be given safely.
No high fever or toxaemia argues strongly against a bacterial airway infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oropharyngeal examination: mild posterior pharyngeal erythema, no tonsillar exudate, midline uvula, no cervical lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild pharyngeal inflammation without exudate or asymmetry is consistent with a viral URI; clinician-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear bilateral chest auscultation, no audible stridor on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear lung fields and no examined stridor argue against lower-airway or tracheal infection; clinician-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Indirect/flexible laryngoscopy: erythematous, oedematous vocal cords without structural lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inflamed oedematous cords with no mass confirm laryngitis and exclude a structural lesion; requires equipment the patient does not operate, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP 122/78, HR 84, RR 16, SpO2 98%, Temp 37.6 C)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal observations with only a low-grade temperature and normal SpO2 confirm a stable, non-toxic patient; measured by the clinician, sealed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine bloods / throat swab / imaging in the acute setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Acute viral laryngitis is a clinical diagnosis; routine investigations are not indicated and over-ordering them is an error of commission.
Reserved for hoarseness failing to resolve after about 14 days, to exclude a structural or malignant vocal-cord lesion — not required acutely.
